name: Band Chest Press
resistance_profile: Accommodating band resistance increases toward lockout, making the press hardest at full extension and easiest near the chest.
cues: Step out to load the band before pressing
Drive the hands forward and together
Keep the ribs down — don't arch to cheat the rep
execution_steps: Anchor a band behind you at upper-back height, or loop it across your back and hold an end in each hand.
Step forward into a staggered stance with the hands at chest level and elbows bent.
Brace the core and set the shoulder blades back and down.
Press the hands forward and slightly together until the arms are extended, squeezing the chest.
Control the hands back to the chest against the band's pull and repeat.
good_when: Home or travel training with only bands
Rebuilding pressing capacity in a shoulder-friendly way
You want a scalable pressing stimulus with no spotter
bad_when: You want to press heavy for low reps
You want maximal load in the stretched chest position
common_errors: Letting the hands drift too low, turning it into a downward push
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears
Starting with too little band tension
progressions: Use a stronger or shorter band
Add reps or a slow eccentric
regressions: Press one arm at a time for more control
